behavior_summary: STALEGG::hatch ensures an egg only hatches once by checking an egg-state id and early-returning if already hatched.

If not already hatched it marks the egg as hatched (Egg::setEggId(...,1)), triggers a camera quake and U8P_STALEGG::hatch sound, spawns a helper process, creates a new item at the egg's X,Y coordinates, flags that created item, and launches/hurls it.
STALEGG::leaveFastArea is a separate handler invoked when the trigger region is left; it clears the egg's quality (Item::setQuality(...,0)), likely disabling or resetting interactions.
notable_control_flow: Guard: a read of Egg::getEggId on the triggering item is compared against 1; if equal the function returns immediately to prevent repeated hatches (strong guard to make U8P_STALEGG::hatch one-shot).
State update happens immediately after the guard: Egg::setEggId(...,1) marks the egg as hatched before spawning/creating effects, preventing reentrancy during the sequence.

The function sequences side effects with engine 'suspend' points around spawn/create/pop/hurl operations (spawn helper process, create/pop item, start a hurl process), indicating asynchronous engine/process synchronization.
leaveFastArea runs independently on area exit and performs cleanup by setting item quality to 0.
open_questions: What exact item ID is created by Item::create/Item::pop (the disassembly shows a pushed id but the concrete item type is not identified here)?
What gameplay semantics does the Item::orStatus mask 0x02 encode for the created object (e.g., 'active', 'thrown', or other state)?
What precise parameters control Item::hurl (direction, force, lifespan)? The numeric args are present but their mapping to gameplay is inferred.
What is the purpose/behavior of the spawned FREE::1C19 process (visual effect, timer, AI for the hatched creature)?

main_function.name: U8P_STALEGG::hatch
main_function.purpose: Handle an egg hatching event: ensure the egg only hatches once, mark it as hatched, produce audiovisual feedback (camera quake + SFX), spawn a helper process, create and flag a new item at the egg's position, and launch that created item.
entry_conditions: Event 7 ('U8P_STALEGG::hatch') — invoked when the egg's U8P_STALEGG::hatch event is triggered.
Appears tied to a fast-area or explicit U8P_STALEGG::hatch trigger (inferred from project fast-area semantics).

function_map.name: U8P_STALEGG::hatch
function_map.role: Primary U8P_STALEGG::hatch event handler for the egg; enforces one-shot U8P_STALEGG::hatch and sequences audiovisual spawn/create/hurl behavior.

function_map.name: U8P_STALEGG::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area exit handler for the egg: clears the egg's quality (Item::setQuality(...,0)) to reset or disable the egg when leaving the trigger region.

function_map.name: Egg::getEggId
function_map.role: Read egg internal ID/state to determine whether the egg has already hatched.

function_map.name: Egg::setEggId
function_map.role: Mark the egg's internal id/state (used to record that it has hatched, value set to 1).

function_map.name: Camera::startQuake
function_map.role: Start camera quake to emphasize U8P_STALEGG::hatch.

function_map.name: spawn (FREE::1C19)
function_map.role: Spawn a helper/free process associated with the U8P_STALEGG::hatch sequence (purpose inferred as visual/timing helper).

function_map.name: Item::create
function_map.role: Allocate/initialize a new item id for placement into the world.

function_map.name: Item::pop
function_map.role: Place/finalize the created item into the world at given coordinates and return a handle.

function_map.name: Item::getX
function_map.role: Get the egg's X coordinate to position the created item.

function_map.name: Item::getY
function_map.role: Get the egg's Y coordinate to position the created item.

function_map.name: Item::orStatus
function_map.role: OR status flags on the created item (mask 0x02 used here).

function_map.name: Item::hurl (process)
function_map.role: Process to launch/throw the created item; invoked with numeric parameters and the created-item handle.

function_map.name: Item::setQuality
function_map.role: Set an item's quality (used by leaveFastArea to set quality to 0).

external_calls.purpose: Check whether the egg has already hatched (guard/one-shot check).
Mark the egg as hatched (set id/state to 1) to prevent repeated hatching.
Begin camera quake effect to emphasize U8P_STALEGG::hatch.
Spawn a helper process to accompany the U8P_STALEGG::hatch sequence (likely visual/timing).
Allocate the item id/slot for whatever is produced by the egg.
Obtain egg Y coordinate so the created item can be placed there.
Obtain egg X coordinate so the created item can be placed there.
Place the created item into the world at the egg's coordinates and receive its handle.
Set flags (mask 0x02) on the created item, presumably to mark it active or ready for physics/interaction.
Stop the camera quake effect once the core spawn/pop sequence is complete.
Start a hurl/launch process to throw/launch the newly created item with specific numeric parameters.
Set the egg's quality to 0 on leaving the trigger region to disable or reset it.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: STALEGG::hatch ensures an egg only hatches once by checking an egg-state id and early-returning if already hatched.
